8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

18	    header = (

21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )

29	        title = b.title[:title_w]

35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:

50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(

60

67

2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

Perfect! I now have a complete understanding of the codebase. Let me provide you with a comprehensive analysis:

---

## Complete Codebase Analysis: Python CLI Bookmark Manager

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

The project has a clean, organized structure:

```
bookmark-manager/
├── pyproject.toml              # Project metadata and dependencies
├── .gitignore                  # Git ignore rules
├── src/
│   ├── __init__.py             # Empty package marker
│   ├── cli.py                  # CLI entry point and command definitions
│   ├── models.py               # Data models (Bookmark class)
│   ├── storage.py              # Persistence layer (JSON-based storage)
│   └── formatter.py            # Output formatting (table and JSON)
└── tests/
    ├── __init__.py
    ├── test_storage.py         # Storage layer tests
    └── test_search.py          # Search feature tests (not yet implemented)
```

**Key Details:**
- Python 3.10+ required
- Entry point: `bookmark = "src.cli:main"` (defined in pyproject.toml)
- Uses pytest for testing
- No external dependencies beyond stdlib

---

### 2. MAIN CLI ENTRY POINT AND COMMANDS

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463616188-3qs4vy/src/cli.py`

The CLI is built with argparse and currently supports 4 commands:

| Command | Purpose | Arguments |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| `add` | Create new bookmark | `url`, `title`, `--tags`, `--notes` |
| `list` | Show all bookmarks | `--format` (table\|json, default: table) |
| `delete` | Remove bookmark | `id` |
| `show` | Display single bookmark | `id` |

**Architecture:**
- `build_parser()` - Creates and configures the argument parser with all subparsers
- `main(argv=None)` - Entry point that parses args and dispatches to handler functions
- Each command is handled with if/elif blocks in the main function
- Uses subparsers for command routing

---

### 3. DATA MODEL FOR BOOKMARKS

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463616188-3qs4vy/src/models.py`

```python
@dataclass
class Bookmark:
    id: str                                    # UUID
    url: str                                   # Full URL
    title: str                                 # Display title
    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # List of tags
    notes: str = ""                            # Optional notes
    created_at: str = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat())  # ISO format timestamp
```

**Methods:**
- `to_dict()` - Converts to dictionary for JSON serialization
- `from_dict(cls, data: dict)` - Class method to deserialize from dictionary

---

### 4. EXISTING COMMANDS IMPLEMENTATION

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463616188-3qs4vy/src/storage.py`

The storage module provides these functions:

| Function | Purpose | Returns |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `load_bookmarks(db_path)` | Read all bookmarks from JSON file | `List[Bookmark]` |
| `save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)` | Write bookmarks to JSON file | `None` |
| `add_bookmark(url, title, tags, notes, db_path)` | Create and persist bookmark | `Bookmark` (with generated UUID) |
| `delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` | Remove bookmark by ID | `bool` (success/failure) |
| `get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` | Retrieve single bookmark | `Optional[Bookmark]` |

**Default Storage Location:** `~/.bookmarks.json`

**Storage Format:** JSON array of objects:
```json
[
  {
    "id": "uuid-string",
    "url": "https://example.com",
    "title": "Example",
    "tags": ["tag1", "tag2"],
    "notes": "Optional notes",
    "created_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00"
  }
]
```

---

### 5. OUTPUT FORMATTING

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463616188-3qs4vy/src/formatter.py`

Two formatters are provided:

**Table Format (`format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str`):**
- Displays as plain-text table with columns: ID, Title, URL, Tags
- Column widths: ID=8, Title=30, URL=40, Tags=20 characters
- IDs truncated to first 8 characters
- Returns "No bookmarks found." when empty
- Example output:
```
ID       Title                          URL                                      Tags
-------- ------------------------------ ---------------------------------------- --------------------
aaa-111  Example Site                   https://example.com                      example, test
```

**JSON Format (`format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str`):**
- Full JSON serialization with 2-space indentation
- Preserves all fields including full IDs and created_at timestamps
- Returns valid JSON that can be parsed by downstream tools

---

### 6. TEST STRUCTURE AND PATTERNS

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463616188-3qs4vy/tests/test_storage.py`

Testing framework: **pytest**

**Test Organization:**
- Test classes group related tests (TestLoadBookmarks, TestSaveBookmarks, etc.)
- Fixtures for test isolation:
  - `db_path(tmp_path)` - Temporary database file per test
  - `sample_bookmarks()` - Reusable test data with 2 sample bookmarks

**Test Patterns:**
- Setup: Use fixtures to create isolated test environments
- Use temporary files for storage tests
- Test both success and failure paths (e.g., missing files, invalid IDs)
- Verify side effects (file creation, data persistence)

**Tests for Storage Layer** (in test_storage.py):
- LoadBookmarks: empty file handling, loading multiple bookmarks
- SaveBookmarks: file creation, JSON validity
- AddBookmark: object creation, persistence, unique ID generation
- DeleteBookmark: successful deletion, non-existent ID handling
- GetBookmark: retrieval by ID, missing ID handling

---

### 7. STORAGE AND PERSISTENCE LAYER

**Implementation Details:**
- **Format:** JSON file stored at `~/.bookmarks.json`
- **Load mechanism:** 
  - Reads JSON array from file
  - Deserializes each object to Bookmark model
  - Returns empty list if file doesn't exist
- **Save mechanism:**
  - Serializes all Bookmark objects to dictionaries
  - Writes as formatted JSON with 2-space indentation
  - Overwrites entire file (not append)
- **Concurrency:** No locking mechanism - not safe for concurrent writes
- **ID generation:** Uses Python's `uuid.uuid4()` converted to string

---

### 8. UTILITY FUNCTIONS AND REUSABLE PATTERNS

**Available for Reuse:**

1. **In storage.py:**
   - `load_bookmarks()` - Already used in list and show commands
   - `get_bookmark()` - Single bookmark retrieval
   - Pattern: All storage operations load → modify → save

2. **In formatter.py:**
   - `format_table()` - Reusable for any filtered results
   - `format_json()` - JSON output for any bookmark list
   - Both accept `List[Bookmark]` making them composable

3. **In models.py:**
   - `Bookmark.from_dict()` and `Bookmark.to_dict()` - Serialization helpers

4. **In cli.py:**
   - `build_parser()` - The subparser pattern for adding new commands
   - Command handlers in `main()` function follow a clear pattern:
     - Parse arguments → Call storage/business logic → Output result

---

### 9. SEARCH FEATURE (NOT YET IMPLEMENTED)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463616188-3qs4vy/tests/test_search.py`

The test file defines the expected behavior for a `search` command that needs to be implemented. Key requirements:

**Expected Functions (need to be created in `src/search.py`):**

1. `search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
   - Case-insensitive substring matching
   - Used by: "Python" should match "Python Tutorial"

2. `search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
   - Exact tag matching (case-sensitive per test)
   - Multiple bookmarks can have same tag

3. `search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
   - Extract domain from URL and match
   - Should handle subdomains: "docs.python.org" matches "https://docs.python.org/3/..."
   - Should handle root domains: "github.com" matches both "github.com/..." and "subdomain.github.com"

4. `search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`
   - Combined search using AND logic for multiple criteria
   - No criteria → returns all bookmarks
   - Conflicting criteria → returns empty list
   - Filters through all three when multiple provided

**Expected CLI Integration:**
- New `search` subcommand with:
  - `--title` optional argument
  - `--tag` optional argument
  - `--domain` optional argument (not shown in test snippet but implied)
  - `--format` option (json or table)
- Parser should accept combinations like:
  - `search --title python` 
  - `search --tag docs --format json`
  - `search --title python --tag docs --domain docs.python.org`

---

## Summary of Key Integration Points for New `search` Command

1. **Create `src/search.py`** with the four functions above
2. **Update `src/cli.py`:**
   - Add search subparser in `build_parser()`
   - Add handler in `main()` function
   - Integrate with `load_bookmarks()` and formatters
3. **Test Pattern:** Tests already exist in test_search.py and expect this implementation
4. **Data Flow:** `load_bookmarks()` → `search()` function → `format_table()` or `format_json()` → print

### **3. Fixtures & Test Helpers**

#### **Pytest Fixtures Used:**

1. **`db_path`** (test_storage.py)
   - Type: Built-in `tmp_path` fixture
   - Purpose: Creates temporary test database file path
   - Returns: String path to a temporary JSON file

2. **`sample_bookmarks`** (test_storage.py)
   - Purpose: Provides 2 pre-configured Bookmark instances for testing
   - Contains: Example bookmarks with realistic data (URLs, tags, notes)

3. **`bookmarks`** (test_search.py)
   - Purpose: Provides 5 diverse Bookmark instances for search testing
   - Contains: Bookmarks across different domains (Python docs, GitHub, MDN, etc.)
   - Covers multiple tags: python, javascript, docs, sdk, runpod, flask, web, mdn, cli

---

### **4. CLI Invocation Testing**

**Method Used:** `argparse` directly (NOT Click CLI runner, NOT subprocess)

The tests use:
```python
from src.cli import build_parser

parser = build_parser()
args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])
```

**Current CLI Commands Tested:**
- `search --title <query>`
- `search --tag <query>`
- `search --format json` (or table)

**Note:** The search command is NOT yet implemented in `src/cli.py` (based on imports in test_search.py). This appears to be a test file for a planned feature.

---

### **5. Test Organization Patterns**

**Class-Based Organization:**
- Tests organized into classes by functionality
- `TestLoadBookmarks`, `TestSaveBookmarks`, `TestAddBookmark`, `TestDeleteBookmark`, `TestGetBookmark` (storage)
- `TestSearchByTitle`, `TestSearchByTag`, `TestSearchByDomain`, `TestSearchCombined`, `TestSearchCLI` (search)

**Naming Conventions:**
- Test methods: `test_<specific_behavior>`
- Classes: `Test<FunctionName>`

---

### **6. Output Testing**

**Storage Tests (test_storage.py):**
- JSON file validation using `json.load()` to verify format
- Direct attribute assertions on returned Bookmark objects

**Search Tests (test_search.py):**
- Tests CLI `--format json` flag acceptance (parser validation only, not actual output formatting)
- No actual formatter output tests in existing test files

**Note:** The `formatter.py` module (with `format_table()` and `format_json()`) has NO dedicated tests yet.

---

### **7. Mocking & Storage Testing Patterns**

**Storage Mocking Strategy:**
- **No mocks used** - tests use actual file I/O with temporary directories
- `tmp_path` pytest fixture provides isolated temporary file system
- Each test gets its own fresh database file path
- Tests verify both: in-memory objects AND persistent file state

**Testing Patterns:**

1. **Round-trip Testing:**
   ```python
   save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
   result = load_bookmarks(db_path)
   assert result[0].id == "aaa-111"
   ```

2. **State Persistence Testing:**
   ```python
   add_bookmark(url="https://a.com", title="A", db_path=db_path)
   bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
   assert len(bookmarks) == 1
   ```

3. **JSON Format Validation:**
   ```python
   save_bookmarks(sample_bookmarks, db_path)
   with open(db_path) as f:
       data = json.load(f)  # Verifies valid JSON
   assert data[0]["url"] == "https://example.com"
   ```

---

### **8. Test Coverage Summary**

**test_storage.py (12 tests):**
- Load functionality: 2 tests
- Save functionality: 2 tests
- Add functionality: 3 tests
- Delete functionality: 2 tests
- Get functionality: 2 tests
- Total: 11 assertions across 12 test methods

**test_search.py (16+ tests):**
- Search by title: 3 tests
- Search by tag: 3 tests
- Search by domain: 3 tests
- Combined search: 7 tests
- CLI integration: 2 tests
- Total: 20+ assertions across 18 test methods

**Overall:** 30+ test methods covering storage I/O, data persistence, search logic, and CLI argument parsing

---

### **9. Data Models Used in Tests**

**Bookmark Model** (imported from `src.models`):
- `id`: UUID string
- `url`: Full URL string
- `title`: Display name
- `tags`: List of strings
- `notes`: Optional description
- `created_at`: ISO format datetime string

**Sample Data Pattern:**
- Tests use realistic example data (real URLs like docs.python.org, github.com, MDN)
- Timestamps are fixed ISO strings (e.g., "2026-01-01T00:00:00")
- Tags cover common document categories: python, docs, tutorial, sdk, etc.

---

### **10. Key Testing Insights**

1. **No External Dependencies:** Tests don't rely on Click, requests, or other HTTP libraries
2. **File-Based Storage:** All tests work with JSON file I/O, no database
3. **Isolation:** Each test gets its own temporary directory via pytest's `tmp_path`
4. **Search Module Incomplete:** test_search.py imports from non-existent `src/search.py` module
5. **CLI Tests are Parser-Only:** No subprocess or actual command execution testing
6. **No Output Validation:** Search CLI tests don't verify actual formatted output
7. **Deterministic Data:** Tests use fixed IDs and timestamps, not random generation

---

This is a well-structured test suite focused on core functionality with room for expansion in CLI integration and output formatting validation.

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

# Plan: Add `search` Command to Bookmark CLI

## Context
The bookmark manager CLI (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter bookmarks. Users need a `search` command that filters by title (substring, case-insensitive), tag (exact match), and domain, applying AND logic when multiple criteria are given. Tests already exist in `tests/test_search.py` and import from a not-yet-created `src/search.py` module. The CLI tests check the argparse parser only (no subprocess calls).

## Files to Modify / Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `src/search.py` | **Create** — all search logic |
| `src/cli.py` | **Edit** — add import, subparser, and handler |

---

## Step 1: Create `src/search.py`

Create a new file with four public functions. Use `urllib.parse.urlparse` (stdlib, already available) for domain extraction.

```python
from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against the bookmark's tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match bookmarks whose URL netloc equals domain or is a subdomain of it."""
    results = []
    for b in bookmarks:
        netloc = urlparse(b.url).netloc
        if netloc == domain or netloc.endswith("." + domain):
            results.append(b)
    return results


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Apply all provided criteria with AND logic. No criteria → all bookmarks."""
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results
```

**Domain matching rationale:**  
- Searching `"github.com"` matches URLs whose netloc is exactly `github.com` (2 bookmarks in test data ✓)  
- Searching `"docs.python.org"` matches URLs whose netloc is exactly `docs.python.org` (2 bookmarks ✓)  
- The `endswith("." + domain)` branch handles future cases like `api.github.com` matching `github.com`

---

## Step 2: Edit `src/cli.py`

### 2a — Add import at the top (alongside existing imports)

```python
from .search import search
```

Add this line after the existing imports (line 5).

### 2b — Add search subparser in `build_parser()` (after `show_parser` block, before `return parser`)

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--title", default=None, help="Filter by title (substring, case-insensitive)"
)
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--tag", default=None, help="Filter by tag (exact match)"
)
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain"
)
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

### 2c — Add handler in `main()` (after the `show` elif block)

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

---

## How the Tests Pass

| Test class | What it tests | Satisfied by |
|---|---|---|
| `TestSearchByTitle` | Case-insensitive substring on `title` | `search_by_title` in `src/search.py` |
| `TestSearchByTag` | Exact match in `tags` list | `search_by_tag` in `src/search.py` |
| `TestSearchByDomain` | netloc equality / subdomain | `search_by_domain` using `urlparse` |
| `TestSearchCombined` | AND logic, no-op when no criteria | `search()` chaining |
| `TestSearchCLI.test_search_command_exists` | `args.command == "search"`, `args.title` | search subparser |
| `TestSearchCLI.test_search_format_json_flag` | `args.tag`, `args.format == "json"` | `--tag` + `--format` args |

---

## Verification

Run all tests:
```bash
pytest tests/ -v
```

Expected: all tests in `test_storage.py` and `test_search.py` pass (18 test methods total in search, 12 in storage).

Manual smoke test (optional):
```bash
bookmark search --title python
bookmark search --tag docs --format json
bookmark search --title python --tag docs --domain docs.python.org
```

# Plan: Add `search` Command to Bookmark CLI

## Context
The bookmark manager CLI (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter bookmarks. Users need a `search` command that filters by title (substring, case-insensitive), tag (exact match), and domain, applying AND logic when multiple criteria are given. Tests already exist in `tests/test_search.py` and import from a not-yet-created `src/search.py` module. The CLI tests check the argparse parser only (no subprocess calls).

## Step 1: Create `src/search.py`

Create a new file with four public functions. Use `urllib.parse.urlparse` (stdlib, already available) for domain extraction.

```python
from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against the bookmark's tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match bookmarks whose URL netloc equals domain or is a subdomain of it."""
    results = []
    for b in bookmarks:
        netloc = urlparse(b.url).netloc
        if netloc == domain or netloc.endswith("." + domain):
            results.append(b)
    return results


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Apply all provided criteria with AND logic. No criteria → all bookmarks."""
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results
```

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json
from .search import search


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)

            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
